statement: Clearly Canadian was founded in 1987 in British Columbia, making it one of Canada's first premium flavoured sparkling water brands — predating the sparkling water category boom by over a decade.
source: Clearly Canadian company history

statement: Clearly Canadian's brand mission is rooted in 'good thoughts, good words, good deeds' — a commitment to positive impact reflected in its sourcing, packaging, and community partnerships.
source: Clearly Canadian brand guidelines

statement: All Clearly Canadian sparkling water is sourced from a British Columbia aquifer, and the brand's tagline 'Clearly it's our Water' reflects the purity and regional authenticity of its source.
source: Clearly Canadian product label and brand website

statement: Clearly Canadian is available in 33,600 retail doors across Canada and the US, representing 69% ACV (All Commodity Volume) distribution — a significant national footprint for an independent Canadian brand.
source: Clearly Canadian retail distribution data 2025

statement: CC Maple is Clearly Canadian's newest line — a naturally maple-flavoured sparkling water positioned both as a standalone drink and as a premium mixer that pairs with bourbon, whiskey, and gin, as well as a sober-curious mocktail base with autumn-inspired appeal.
source: Clearly Canadian CC Maple product launch communications 2025

statement: Clearly Canadian Originals are available in four signature flavours: Mountain Blackberry, Wild Cherry, Orchard Peach, and Summer Strawberry — each lightly sweetened and made with no artificial colours or preservatives.
source: Clearly Canadian product listing 2025

statement: Clearly Canadian Originals contain approximately 80 calories and 20g of cane sugar per 325ml glass bottle, positioning the product as a lightly sweetened sparkling water rather than a zero-calorie alternative.
source: Clearly Canadian Originals product label

statement: The Clearly Canadian Originals glass bottle features a distinctive curved, embossed design inspired by the shape of a baseball glove — an iconic form that has remained largely unchanged since the 1980s and drives immediate shelf recognition.
source: Brand packaging history and design documentation

statement: Clearly Canadian Originals received the InnoBev 'Best in Glass' award, recognizing the brand's iconic glass bottle format as a standout packaging innovation in the global beverage industry.
source: InnoBev Awards — Clearly Canadian

statement: Clearly Canadian SleekCans are sold in 6-packs at approximately $7.99 — roughly 50% less per serving than the glass bottle format — making them the entry-level price point for impulse and convenience channel shoppers.
source: Clearly Canadian SleekCan retail pricing 2025

statement: Clearly Canadian SleekCans were introduced specifically to expand distribution into convenience and impulse channels where glass bottles are impractical, broadening the brand's reach beyond grocery and specialty retail.
source: Clearly Canadian channel strategy documentation

statement: Clearly Canadian Zero Sugar uses stevia leaf extract as its natural sweetener, delivering zero grams of sugar per bottle with the same fruit-forward flavour profile as the Originals.
source: Clearly Canadian Zero Sugar product label

statement: Clearly Canadian Zero Sugar launched in 2024 and is currently distributed primarily in the US market, with limited availability in Canada — the brand's support and marketing investment behind this line remains selective.
source: Clearly Canadian distribution and launch communications 2024

statement: Clearly Canadian Essence is the brand's unsweetened sparkling water line — zero sweeteners, zero calories — targeted at health-first consumers who want flavour without any sugar or sugar substitutes.
source: Clearly Canadian Essence product communications

statement: Clearly Canadian Essence is available in grapefruit and limon flavours, both using natural fruit essences to deliver a clean, crisp taste with no sweeteners of any kind.
source: Clearly Canadian Essence product listing 2025

statement: Clearly Canadian Sparkling Mineral water retains natural minerals sourced from the BC aquifer, and is positioned as a premium, on-premise product — suited for restaurants, bars, and fine dining rather than retail mass market.
source: Clearly Canadian Sparkling Mineral product and channel documentation

statement: CC Maple is available in both the signature Clearly Canadian glass bottle and SleekCan format, allowing on-premise accounts (bars, restaurants) to serve it by the glass while enabling retail and convenience takeaway.
source: Clearly Canadian CC Maple launch documentation 2025

statement: CC Maple launched with an on-premise-first distribution strategy, seeding the product in bars and cocktail-forward venues before expanding to retail — designed to build bartender advocacy and cocktail association before mass shelf placement.
source: Clearly Canadian CC Maple go-to-market strategy 2025

statement: CC Maple is designed as a sober-curious mocktail base, with its natural maple flavor and light carbonation making it a versatile non-alcoholic ingredient for autumn-inspired drinks and zero-proof cocktail menus.
source: Clearly Canadian CC Maple brand and bartender communications 2025

statement: Clearly Canadian uses only glass bottles and aluminum cans across its entire product portfolio — no plastic packaging — reinforcing its sustainability positioning and differentiating from single-use plastic sparkling water brands.
source: Clearly Canadian sustainability messaging and packaging documentation

statement: Clearly Canadian is carried at major Canadian and US retail partners including Whole Foods, Loblaws, Safeway Canada, and specialty grocery chains — with the glass bottle format anchoring the premium grocery tier.
source: Clearly Canadian retail distribution data 2025

statement: Clearly Canadian achieved $65.8 million in US retail sales with +39.4% year-over-year growth, making it one of the fastest-growing premium sparkling water brands in the US market.
source: Clearly Canadian retail sales data 2024–2025

statement: Clearly Canadian's 2015 relaunch was powered by a grassroots crowdfunding campaign that attracted 14,000 fans from 35 countries who pre-ordered 40,000 cases — proving that deep consumer nostalgia could restart a dormant brand.
source: Clearly Canadian relaunch press coverage and campaign documentation 2015

statement: In 2025, Clearly Canadian announced a partnership with the Toronto Blue Jays, aligning the brand with Canada's MLB franchise and expanding its presence in sports and entertainment venues across the country.
source: Clearly Canadian Blue Jays partnership announcement 2025
